rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: COMPLIANCE WITH ADMINISTRATIVE SUBPOENA.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line 1. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: COMPLIANCE WITH ADMINISTRATIVE SUBPOENA. Continued on the court's own motion to December 16, 2025. Moving party has not filed proof of service of the order to show cause and moving papers as required by the court's order of October 8, 2025. Briefing dates are as set in the October 8 order, calculated from the continued hearing date. Moving party shall provide notice of the continued hearing date to responding party and file proof of service with the court not later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: SPIRE GLOBAL SUBSIDIARY, INC. VS. ASTRA SPACE OPERATIONS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line 4. PLAINTIFF SPIRE GLOBAL SUBSIDIARY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff Spire Global Subsidiary, Inc. ("Spire") moves for summary judgment on its single cause of action for breach of contract against defendant Astra Space Operations, LLC ("Astra"). Spire's motion is granted. The court makes no determination about attorneys' fees in this motion. (See Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.1702.) The court concludes Spire is entitled to judgment on its breach of contract cause of action in the amount of $400,000. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: SANDRA PORTER VS. ZACKS & FREEDMAN, PC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Third Party Dr. Khaira To Produce Documents And Appear For Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANT ZACKS & FREEDMAN, P.C.'S Motion To Compel Third Party Dr. Khaira To Produce Documents And Appear For Deposition. Defendant's unopposed motion to compel third party Dr. Jagmohan Khaira to appear for deposition is granted. Although Dr. Khaira appears to have provided documents and a custodian declaration, the deposition subpoena served on him requires testimony. (Sprague Decl., Ex. C.) The court orders Dr. Khaira to appear on a mutually agreed date for deposition within 45 days of entry of this order. A deposition subpoena is tantamount to an order of the court to appear for deposition. The court concludes that sanctions are warranted here and orders Dr. Khaira to pay $2,950.55 to Zacks & Freedman, P.C. within 30 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line 9. DEFENDANTS DC UTAH SUB LLC, COMSTOCK UTAH STREET LLC'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Plaintiff failed to respond to defendants' meet and confer efforts concerning the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint. (See Sosnick Decl., para. 4.) The court continues the matter for hearing to December 15, 2025. Plaintiff is ordered to meet and confer by telephone or in person and to submit a declaration showing compliance prior to the continued hearing date. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 430.41, subd. (a)(1).) Plaintiff has leave to amend to cure any deficiencies prior to the continued hearing date. The meet and confer obligation is mandatory. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: PRECILA BALABBO VS. TARTE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line1 2. PLAINTIFF PRECILA BALABBO'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and revised consent judgment. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14) in the event defendant does not reformulate; that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. The parties may submit the proposed revised consent judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: REBECCA BUNTING VS. THE REALREAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line 13. DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. The unopposed motion to compel arbitration and stay this action is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MARIVIC HUFF VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line 14. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Stay. The unopposed motion to stay this case pending resolution of the federal vaccine cases is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: QUEST BLUE LLC ET AL VS. MICHAEL MAMONE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 18, 2025, line 15. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. Plaintiffs Quest Blue LLC and Mission III LLC's motion for preliminary injunction is granted in part and denied in part. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
